issues: palette_mismatch: candidate is white/cyan/light-blue; peers are dark charcoal/gunmetal/warm-bronze — near-zero palette overlap
color_temperature: peers trend warm-dark; candidate is cold-bright, entirely opposite temperature register
outline_color: peers use near-black dark outlines consistently; candidate outline reads as mid-gray to white on bright field — breaks family rule
highlight_intensity: candidate highlights are blown-out white, creating a glowing/ethereal look; peers have restrained specular pops on metal
shadow_count_and_direction: peers show 1–2 tight drop shadows anchoring volume; candidate's circular form uses diffuse ambient glow with no consistent directional cue
rendering_style: peers are grounded pixel-metal with hard shadow termination; candidate reads as ethereal portal/UI-badge — different visual vocabulary entirely
charm_level: peers are cute-tough armour items with menacing silhouettes; candidate looks like a minimap icon or UI widget, not a sibling object in this family
reasoning: Across 7 of 11 assessed dimensions the candidate departs significantly from the peer family. The most damaging breaks are palette and color-temperature: peers live in a warm-dark metallic world; scroll_teleport_v01 is cold, bright, and white-dominant with cyan tones that appear in none of the reference sprites. Outline color and weight are secondary but consistent violations. Shadow handling is diffuse rather than directional. Even accounting for item-type difference (scroll vs helmet), the rendering vocabulary is simply not the same. Confidence is high because the mismatch is large and perceptually obvious at a glance.
